title: DAST and SAST Finding Density Before Payment Service Releases

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 6.3.2 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 6.3.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.38: DAST and SAST Finding Density Before Payment Service Releases.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.38' running on index=devsecops sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan") — app_name, build_id, severity, status, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=devsecops sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan") earliest=-30d
| lookup pci_payment_apps.csv app_name OUTPUT pci_app
| where pci_app="true"
| where severity IN ("High","Very High","Critical")
| where match(status,"(?i)open|new)")
| stats count by app_name, build_id, scan_type
| where count>0
| sort - count
description: Tracks high-severity static and dynamic scan findings per build of payment APIs so security testing is evidenced prior to promoting code that could affect account data.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes devsecops and sourcetypes checkmarx:scan, veracode:scan. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Map scanner project names to internal `app_name`. (2) Gate release on `count=0` for Critical if policy mandates. (3) Correlate with UC-22.11.37 for same `build_id`. (4) Quarterly trending for PCI interview. Indexes required: `index=devsecops`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=checkmarx:scan`, `sourcetype=veracode:scan`; Lookups: `pci_payment_apps`, `pci_payment_apps.csv`.
visualization: Stacked bar (open highs by app), table (build_id), line (count over time).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t dc(Vulnerabilities.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Scan failures during legacy system lifecycle, documented compensating controls, or planned upgrade windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom `sourcetype="veracode:scan"` / `checkmarx:scan"` HEC normalizers.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=devsecops` `sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan")` — `app_name`, `build_id`, `severity`, `status`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map scanner project names to internal `app_name`. (2) Gate release on `count=0` for Critical if policy mandates. (3) Correlate with UC-22.11.37 for same `build_id`. (4) Quarterly trending for PCI interview.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devsecops sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan") earliest=-30d
| lookup pci_payment_apps.csv app_name OUTPUT pci_app
| where pci_app="true"
| where severity IN ("High","Very High","Critical")
| where match(status,"(?i)open|new)")
| stats count by app_name, build_id, scan_type
| where count>0
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**DAST and SAST Finding Density Before Payment Service Releases (PCI DSS Req 6.3.1, 6.3.2)** — Tracks high-severity static and dynamic scan findings per build of payment APIs so security testing is evidenced prior to promoting code that could affect account data.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=devsecops` `sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan")` — `app_name`, `build_id`, `severity`, `status`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom `sourcetype="veracode:scan"` / `checkmarx:scan"` HEC normalizers. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devsecops.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=devsecops, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where pci_app="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where severity IN ("High","Very High","Critical")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(status,"(?i)open|new)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by app_name, build_id, scan_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (open highs by app), table (build_id), line (count over time).
grandmaExplanation: We track high-severity static and dynamic scan findings per build of payment APIs so security testing is evidenced prior to promoting code that could affect account data. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Vulnerabilities (CIM data model).
